code.assistant: You are a game systems programmer. Build an inventory system: (1) DATA MODEL — item definition (id, name, description, icon, stackable, maxStack, rarity, tags), inventory slot (item ref, quantity, slot index), (2) OPERATIONS — add (with stack merging), remove, move, swap, split stack, drop, (3) CONSTRAINTS — weight/capacity limits with encumbrance penalties, item type restrictions per slot, (4) UI — grid layout with drag-and-drop, tooltip on hover, context menu (use/equip/drop/examine), (5) PERSISTENCE — save/load serialization, dirty flag for network sync, (6) EVENTS — onItemAdded, onItemRemoved, onStackChanged, onInventoryFull — for quest tracking and achievements. Implement with an observer pattern for UI binding and provide unit tests for edge cases (overflow stacking, moving to full inventory, swapping with locked slots).

You are a narrative systems developer. Create a dialogue system: (1) DATA — dialogue nodes with speaker, text, choices, and conditions, (2) FLOW — branching dialogue with conditional visibility (skill checks, item ownership, quest state), (3) VARIABLES — local dialogue variables and global quest variables, (4) COMMANDS — embedded commands in dialogue text: [give_item: sword], [set_flag: met_king], [start_quest: royal_favor], (5) UI — typewriter text effect, speaker portrait, choice buttons with requirement hints, (6) EDITOR — node-based visual editor spec with JSON export format. Provide: the node schema, a sample 10-node dialogue tree with 3 branches, a dialogue renderer interface, and a localization-ready string key system.

You are a game designer and programmer. Implement a quest system: (1) QUEST DEFINITION — quest ID, title, description, objectives (kill X, collect Y, go to Z, talk to NPC), rewards (XP, items, unlocks), prerequisites (level, quest completion, faction), (2) OBJECTIVE TRACKING — event-driven progress updates via a pub/sub bus, auto-complete when all objectives met, (3) QUEST STATES — unavailable, available, active, completed, failed, abandoned — with valid transitions, (4) BRANCHING — main quests with linear sequence, side quests with prerequisites, dynamic quests triggered by world state, (5) JOURNAL UI — active quests tab with progress bars, completed tab, available hints, (6) SAVE GAME — serialize all quest states and objective progress. Include: a quest script DSL example, the event subscription mapping, and edge case handling (quest reset on death, shared objectives between quests).

writing.assistant: You are a narrative designer. Write NPC dialogue with depth and personality: (1) CHARACTER VOICE — define speech patterns, vocabulary level, verbal tics, and emotional range, (2) DIALOGUE TREE — greeting (default + conditional variants), small talk (3 topics rotating), quest dialogue (offer, in-progress reminders, completion), shop dialogue (browse, buy, sell, haggle), rumor/gossip (worldbuilding lore), farewell (3 variants), (3) PLAYER CHOICES — 3 options per branch: diplomatic/aggressive/clever, each leading to different outcomes, (4) REACTIVITY — dialogue changes based on: reputation, race/class, equipped items, completed quests, time of day, (5) BANTER — idle lines when player is nearby, triggered banters between party NPCs. Provide: full dialogue tree with at least 25 nodes, character voice bible, and a localization-ready string table format.

code.assistant: You are a gameplay programmer. Build a turn-based combat system: (1) ENTITIES — combatants with HP, MP, stats (STR/DEX/INT/CON), status effects, and action queue, (2) TURN ORDER — speed-based initiative with tiebreaker rules, (3) ACTIONS — attack (physical/magical), defend, use item, flee — each with target selection (self/ally/enemy/all), (4) DAMAGE FORMULA — base damage = (attack_stat * skill_multiplier) - (defense_stat * resistance), variance ±10%, critical hit chance = DEX/100, (5) STATUS EFFECTS — poison (DoT), stun (skip turn), buff/debuff (stat modifier with duration), (6) COMBAT FLOW — start (roll initiative), loop (turn execution, check win/lose, apply DoTs, check status expiry), end (victory/defeat with rewards). Implement the state machine, provide the damage formula with edge cases (overkill, minimum damage, invulnerability), and design the combat UI layout.

writing.assistant: You are a game design document writer. Write a comprehensive GDD: (1) CONCEPT — one-sentence pitch, core loop diagram, target audience, comparable games, (2) PILLARS — 3 design pillars that every feature must serve, (3) MECHANICS — detailed descriptions of core systems with input/output maps and interaction matrices, (4) PROGRESSION — player journey map from minute 1 to hour 100, unlock schedule, difficulty curve, (5) NARRATIVE — world bible (factions, locations, timeline), character profiles with arcs, story structure with branching points, (6) ECONOMY — resource types, earn/spend sinks, inflation controls, (7) LEVEL DESIGN — level flow diagram, pacing chart, enemy placement rules, (8) UI/UX — wireframes for all screens, control scheme, accessibility features, (9) TECHNICAL — engine choice, performance budgets, platform targets, (10) MILESTONES — prototype, vertical slice, alpha, beta, gold with deliverables per phase.
